summary:
The patient has a history of hepatitis C exposure as indicated by Anti-HBc Ag IgG positivity; however, absence of HBsAg or HCV RNA confirms non-active viral replication at present. Further evaluation should focus on identifying other causes contributing to hepatic dysfunction such as alcohol use or medications known to affect liver function tests like AST/ALT levels being mildly raised while ALP/GGT remain within normal range suggesting possible secondary biliary injury from pre-existing cirrhotic changes seen via ultrasound findings consistent with chronic liver disease progression towards end-stage organ failure requiring close monitoring including regular follow-ups and possibly therapeutic interventions targeting underlying etiologies beyond just addressing potential reactivation risk posed solely due to prior seroconversion status alone post-exposure period completion.
